what: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: self-contained spec. Drop root tidyusmacro package, ask Claude Code “follow CLAUDE_dallas_pce.md add Dallas trimmed-mean PCE data function.” adds: dallasTrimPCEcomponents — 177-component dictionary mapping Dallas Fed tech-notes list BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U series codes line numbers. getDallasTrimPCE() — panel-builder exposes raw inputs trimmed-mean rate (price, nominal, quantity, weight, price change, trim flag) users can replicate rate component- level analysis. PR ready go end--end human---loop steps — embedded CSV validated mapping (177 rows post-2001 BEA merge).

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: new package dependencies. LazyData: true DESCRIPTION already takes care loading data object.
code: R/   data-dallasTrimPCEcomponents.R   # roxygen-only data doc (NEW)   getDallasTrimPCE.R               # NEW data/   dallasTrimPCEcomponents.rda      # built by data-raw script (NEW) data-raw/   dallasTrimPCEcomponents.R        # build script (NEW)   dallas_trim_pce_components.csv   # source CSV (NEW)

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: Dallas Fed’s trimmed-mean PCE inflation rate computed month fixed list narrow PCE components: durables (1–41), nondurables (42–91), services (92–177), single NPISH aggregate (178). 2009 tech notes (Dolmas, “Trimmed Mean PCE Inflation,” updated 2022-12-23) list 178 components; BEA later combined two single line Table 2.4.4U disaggregated series ended December 2001, working component count 177. Atkinson, Dolmas, Zarutskie (2026) confirm 177.

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: /Users/mtkonczal/Documents/command_line_AI_projects/median_pce_inflation/dallas_components_178.csv exists, copy data-raw/dallas_trim_pce_components.csv rename columns: SeriesCode → series_code, LineNo → line_no. Re-run script BEA renames restructures Table 2.4.4U .

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: Run : Rscript data-raw/dallasTrimPCEcomponents.R.
code: library(readr) library(dplyr)  dallasTrimPCEcomponents <- read_csv(   "data-raw/dallas_trim_pce_components.csv",   col_types = cols(     dallas_idx  = col_integer(),     name        = col_character(),     series_code = col_character(),     line_no     = col_integer(),     bea_label   = col_character()   ) ) |> arrange(dallas_idx)  stopifnot(   nrow(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) == 177,   !any(is.na(dallasTrimPCEcomponents$series_code)),   !any(is.na(dallasTrimPCEcomponents$line_no)),   !any(duplicated(dallasTrimPCEcomponents$line_no)),   93L %in% dallasTrimPCEcomponents$dallas_idx,   !(94L %in% dallasTrimPCEcomponents$dallas_idx) )  usethis::use_data(dallasTrimPCEcomponents, overwrite = TRUE)

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: Mirror style existing R/data-cesDiffusionIndex.R. Roxygen content:
code: #' Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary #' #' A tibble mapping each of the 177 components used in the Federal Reserve #' Bank of Dallas's trimmed-mean PCE inflation rate to its corresponding #' series in BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U (Fisher price index for personal #' consumption expenditures by type of product, monthly). #' #' Components are organized as durables (1-41), nondurables (42-91), #' services (92-177), and one NPISH aggregate (178). The 2009 tech notes #' (Dolmas, "Trimmed Mean PCE Inflation," updated 2022-12-23) list 178 #' components; BEA combined two of them — Tenant-Occupied Stationary #' Homes and Tenant Landlord Durables — into a single line of Table #' 2.4.4U (`IA000629`) when the disaggregated series stopped in December #' 2001. The mapping reflects that combination, yielding 177 rows; #' `dallas_idx` runs 1..178 with 94 omitted (93 holds the merged item). #' #' @format A tibble with 177 rows and 5 variables: #' \describe{ #'   \item{dallas_idx}{Integer. Ordinal position in the Dallas Fed tech-notes list.} #'   \item{name}{Character. Component name as published by the Dallas Fed.} #'   \item{series_code}{Character. BEA NIPA series code in Table 2.4.4U.} #'   \item{line_no}{Integer. Line number in BEA Table 2.4.4U.} #'   \item{bea_label}{Character. Label BEA publishes alongside `series_code`.} #' } #' #' @source #' Dolmas, J. (2009, updated 2022-12-23). "PCE Inflation: Technical #' Note." Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas. BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U. #' #' @references #' Atkinson, T., Dolmas, J., & Zarutskie, R. (2026). "Skewness warrants #' caution as Trimmed Mean PCE inflation eases." Federal Reserve Bank of #' Dallas, April 16, 2026. #' #' @examples #' data(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) #' head(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) #' "dallasTrimPCEcomponents"

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: full file /Users/mtkonczal/Documents/command_line_AI_projects/median_pce_inflation/getDallasTrimPCE.R. Copy verbatim R/. depends getNIPAFiles() (already package) dallasTrimPCEcomponents (added §1). Public signature: Returns long tibble one row per (date, component) columns date, dallas_idx, name, series_code, line_no, price, nominal, quantity, price_change, weight, is_trimmed, trim_side. See roxygen file full details.
code: getDallasTrimPCE(frequency = "M", NIPA_data = NULL,                  alpha = 0.24, beta = 0.31, components = NULL)

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: files added build script run (producing data/dallasTrimPCEcomponents.rda), run R session package root: stopifnot fails, commit — surface failure stop.
code: devtools::document() devtools::load_all()  # Schema & coverage data(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) stopifnot(   inherits(dallasTrimPCEcomponents, "tbl_df"),   nrow(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) == 177,   identical(names(dallasTrimPCEcomponents),             c("dallas_idx","name","series_code","line_no","bea_label")),   93L %in% dallasTrimPCEcomponents$dallas_idx,   !(94L %in% dallasTrimPCEcomponents$dallas_idx),   !any(duplicated(dallasTrimPCEcomponents$line_no)) )  # Live join against BEA — every series_code/line_no must resolve in U20404 nipa <- getNIPAFiles(type = "M") joined_pce <- dplyr::filter(nipa, TableId == "U20404") |>   dplyr::distinct(SeriesCode, LineNo) |>   dplyr::inner_join(dallasTrimPCEcomponents,                     by = c("SeriesCode" = "series_code",                            "LineNo"     = "line_no")) stopifnot(nrow(joined_pce) == 177)  # Function output shape and full-coverage guarantee panel_pce <- getDallasTrimPCE(NIPA_data = nipa) stopifnot(   inherits(panel_pce, "tbl_df"),   all(c("date","dallas_idx","name","series_code","line_no","price",         "nominal","quantity","price_change","weight",         "is_trimmed","trim_side") %in% names(panel_pce)),   panel_pce |>     dplyr::filter(date == max(date), !is.na(weight)) |>     nrow() == 177 ) cat("Dallas PCE: OK\n")  # Package check devtools::check()

what: Things to watch for / non-obvious bits
title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
text: alphabetize dictionary. dallas_idx meaningful (durables / nondurables / services ranges drive category classification downstream code). Disambiguate line/series. BEA Table 2.4.4U, DGASRG used Gasoline & Motor Fuel Natural Gas. Dallas mapping disambiguates line number — joins fresh NIPA pull (series_code, line_no), series_code alone. bea_label informational . Joins (series_code, line_no), labels — BEA edits labels without renumbering. Weights computed inside function. getDallasTrimPCE() builds Fisher (t-1, t) weights data month; nothing needs shipped dictionary. Don’t ship rate-level helper PR. Computing published 12-month trimmed-mean PCE rate (fractional boundary handling Jan-2009 normalization) separate, larger change. PR panel-builder + dictionary — users can build whichever rate want panel. download_pce.R median_pce_inflation project part PR. lives user’s analysis code, package.

title: tidyusmacro update — Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component dataset and helper
code: Add dallasTrimPCEcomponents dataset and getDallasTrimPCE() panel-builder    - dallasTrimPCEcomponents: 177-component dictionary mapping the     Dallas Fed tech-notes list to BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U series codes     and line numbers (with the post-2001 BEA merge of Tenant-Occupied     Stationary Homes + Tenant Landlord Durables).   - getDallasTrimPCE(): long panel with price, nominal, quantity,     Fisher weight, monthly price change, trim flag, and tail label —     enough to replicate the rate or do component-level analysis.

title: Get started with tidyusmacro
text: getBLSFiles() downloads processes data Bureau Labor Statistics flat files. Supports CPI, ECI, JOLTS, CPS, CES, CEX (Consumer Expenditure Survey). BLS asks email address user agent flat-file downloads. getCESRevisions() downloads revisions table Current Employment Survey (CES) total jobs numbers straight BLS website.
code: cpi_data <- getBLSFiles(data_source = "cpi", email = "user@example.com") jolts_data <- getBLSFiles(data_source = "jolts", email = "user@example.com") revisions_df <- getCESRevisions()

title: Get started with tidyusmacro
text: getNIPAFiles() downloads formats BEA NIPA data flat files, either monthly quarterly values. getPCEInflation() loads processes Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation data weights growth measures. getDallasTrimPCE() builds component-level panel underlying Dallas Fed Trimmed Mean PCE inflation rate: monthly price changes, Fisher expenditure-share weights, flags components trimmed month. Useful replicating trimmed-mean rate analyzing gets trimmed.
code: nipa_quarterly <- getNIPAFiles(type = "Q") nipa_monthly <- getNIPAFiles(type = "M") pce_monthly <- getPCEInflation("M") pce_quarterly <- getPCEInflation("Q") # Default 24/31 Dallas Fed trim panel <- getDallasTrimPCE()  # Replicate the monthly trimmed-mean rate panel |>   dplyr::filter(!is_trimmed) |>   dplyr::group_by(date) |>   dplyr::summarize(trim_pce = weighted.mean(price_change, weight))

title: Get started with tidyusmacro
text: getFRED() downloads merges economic data series Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) API. getUnrateFRED() convenience function download unemployment level labor force FRED calculate unemployment rate.
code: # Named arguments give friendly column names fred_data <- getFRED(prime_epop = "LNS12300060", cpi = "CPIAUCSL")  # Unnamed arguments use lowercase ticker as column name fred_data <- getFRED("UNRATE", "PAYEMS") unrate_data <- getUnrateFRED()

title: Get started with tidyusmacro
text: logLinearProjection() performs log-linear projections historical data. Designed use within dplyr verbs.
code: library(dplyr)  data %>%   mutate(projection = logLinearProjection(     date = date,     value = gdp,     start_date = "2015-01-01",     end_date = "2019-12-01"   ))

title: Get started with tidyusmacro
text: theme_esp() custom ggplot2 theme Economic Security Project graphics cream background clean styling. scale_color_esp() scale_fill_esp() provide ESP-branded color scales ggplot2. date_breaks_gg() creates intelligent date breaks ggplot2 always include last data point. date_breaks_n() generates evenly spaced date breaks selecting every nth unique date.
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(data, aes(date, value)) +   geom_line(color = esp_navy) +   theme_esp() ggplot(data, aes(date, value, color = category)) +   geom_line() +   scale_color_esp() +   theme_esp() ggplot(data, aes(date, value)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_date(breaks = date_breaks_gg(n = 6, last = max(data$date))) ggplot(data, aes(date, value)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_date(breaks = date_breaks_n(data$date, n = 6))

what: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data
title: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data
text: Utilities retrieve tidy U.S. macroeconomic data series public government data providers. Functions streamline access series Bureau Labor Statistics (BLS) full data flat files popular releases like employment inflation, Bureau Economic Analysis National Income Product Accounts (NIPA) tables give GDP related accounts, Federal Reserve Bank St. Louis Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED). return consistent, tidy data frames ready modeling graphics. tools pull entire flat files corresponding set, makes useful exploring data, -depth research, also real-time analysis following releases. BLS BEA pulls updated right go live. (FRED usually updated 40 minutes later.) Though note jobs numbers can take 5-10 minutes right launch time; API calls might work better. package also includes helpers date alignment, log-linear projections, common macro diagnostics, along convenience plot builders quick publication-quality charts R tidyverse’s ggplot2 format.

title: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("tidyusmacro")  # Or the development version from GitHub devtools::install_github("mtkonczal/tidyusmacro")

title: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data
text: see package can , read Get started guide (vignette("tidyusmacro")). Every function documented reference index.
code: library(tidyusmacro)  # The full CPI flat file, tidied, with lookup tables joined cpi <- getBLSFiles(data_source = "cpi", email = "user@example.com")  # FRED series with friendly column names fred_data <- getFRED(prime_epop = "LNS12300060", cpi = "CPIAUCSL")

title: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data
text: library distributed MIT License.

what: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
text: tibble mapping 177 components used Federal Reserve Bank Dallas's trimmed-mean PCE inflation rate corresponding series BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U (Fisher price index personal consumption expenditures type product, monthly).

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
code: dallasTrimPCEcomponents

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
text: tibble 177 rows 5 variables: dallas_idx Integer. Ordinal position Dallas Fed tech-notes list. name Character. Component name published Dallas Fed. series_code Character. BEA NIPA series code Table 2.4.4U. line_no Integer. Line number BEA Table 2.4.4U. bea_label Character. Label BEA publishes alongside series_code.

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
text: Dolmas, J. (2009, updated 2022-12-23). "PCE Inflation: Technical Note." Federal Reserve Bank Dallas. BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U.

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
text: Components organized durables (1-41), nondurables (42-91), services (92-177), one NPISH aggregate (178). 2009 tech notes (Dolmas, "Trimmed Mean PCE Inflation," updated 2022-12-23) list 178 components; BEA combined two - Tenant-Occupied Stationary Homes Tenant Landlord Durables - single line Table 2.4.4U (IA000629) disaggregated series stopped December 2001. mapping reflects combination, yielding 177 rows; dallas_idx runs 1..178 94 omitted (93 holds merged item).

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
text: Atkinson, T., Dolmas, J., & Zarutskie, R. (2026). "Skewness warrants caution Trimmed Mean PCE inflation eases." Federal Reserve Bank Dallas, April 16, 2026.

title: Dallas Fed Trimmed-Mean PCE component dictionary — dallasTrimPCEcomponents
code: data(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) head(dallasTrimPCEcomponents) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   dallas_idx name               series_code line_no bea_label          #>        <int> <chr>              <chr>         <int> <chr>              #> 1          1 New Domestic Autos DNDCRG            7 New domestic autos #> 2          2 New Foreign Autos  DNFCRG            8 New foreign autos  #> 3          3 New Light Trucks   DNWTRG            9 New light trucks   #> 4          4 Used Autos         DNPURG           13 Used autos         #> 5          5 Used Light Trucks  DUTRRG           17 Used light trucks  #> 6          6 Tires              DTATRG           21 Tires

what: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
title: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
text: Create breaks function scale_x_date() always includes last actual data month selects every nth month counting backward.

title: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
code: date_breaks_gg(n = 6, last, decreasing = FALSE)

title: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
text: n Integer; keep every n-th month counting backward last. Default 6. last Date; last (max) date data. Required ensure break placed actual data. decreasing Logical; TRUE, return breaks descending order. Default FALSE.

title: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
text: function usable scale_x_date(breaks = ...).

title: Date breaks anchored to last data month (for ggplot) — date_breaks_gg
code: # Minimal reproducible example (avoid using the name `df`, which masks stats::df) set.seed(1) dat <- data.frame(   date  = seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "month", length.out = 24),   value = cumsum(rnorm(24)) )  library(ggplot2)  ggplot(dat, aes(date, value)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_date(     date_labels = "%b\n%Y",     breaks = date_breaks_gg(n = 6, last = max(dat$date))   ) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)

title: Create evenly spaced breaks — date_breaks_n
text: Generate sequence date breaks ggplot scales, taking every nth unique date.

title: Create evenly spaced breaks — date_breaks_n
code: date_breaks_n(dates, n = 6, decreasing = TRUE)

title: Create evenly spaced breaks — date_breaks_n
text: dates vector dates. n Integer, keep every n-th date (default = 6). decreasing Logical, TRUE (default) sorts dates descending order.

title: Create evenly spaced breaks — date_breaks_n
text: vector dates suitable use ggplot2 axis breaks. default decreasing = TRUE, first (recent) date always included, breaks anchored last observation.

title: Create evenly spaced breaks — date_breaks_n
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  df <- tibble(   date = seq.Date(as.Date("2020-01-01"), as.Date("2025-01-01"), by = "month"),   value = rnorm(61) )  ggplot(df, aes(date, value)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_date(breaks = date_breaks_n(df$date, 6))

what: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
text: Custom ggplot2 theme discrete color/fill scales Economic Security Project graphics.

title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
code: theme_esp(base_family = "Public Sans")  scale_color_esp(...)  scale_fill_esp(...)  scale_colour_esp(...)

title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
text: base_family Base font family theme. Defaults "Public Sans"; font installed, ggplot2 falls back default sans font. ... Passed underlying ggplot2 scale functions (scale_color_manual / scale_fill_manual).

title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
text: theme_esp() returns theme object; scale functions return ggplot2 scale objects. added plot +.

title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
text: theme_esp() builds theme_minimal ESP house style. Note several opinionated defaults: cream plot background ("#f4f2e4"), minor gridlines, axis titles legend removed entirely. Label lines directly (e.g., annotate() geom_text()) re-enable legend theme(legend.position = ...) theme_esp(). scale_color_esp() scale_fill_esp() apply esp_pal palette (three colors, three discrete levels). scale_colour_esp() alias.

title: ESP Theme and Color Scales — esp_theme
code: # Plots are assigned rather than printed: rendering requires the # "Public Sans" font, which check machines may not have registered. library(ggplot2)  p1 <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy / pop)) +   geom_line(color = esp_navy) +   labs(     title = "Unemployment share of population",     caption = "Source: FRED via ggplot2::economics."   ) +   theme_esp()  # Multiple series (palette has 3 colors, so at most 3 levels); # theme_esp() removes the legend, so label lines directly dat <- subset(economics_long, variable %in% c("psavert", "uempmed", "unemploy")) p2 <- ggplot(dat, aes(date, value01, color = variable)) +   geom_line() +   scale_color_esp() +   theme_esp()  # print(p1) or print(p2) to render (needs the font installed)

what: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
text: Downloads processes data Bureau Labor Statistics (BLS) flat files. Supports multiple data sources including CPI, ECI, JOLTS, CPS, CES, others. function retrieves main data file along associated metadata files, merges , returns tidy tibble ready analysis.

title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
code: getBLSFiles(data_source, email)

title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
text: data_source Character string specifying BLS data source. Available options: "cpi" Consumer Price Index - current data "eci" Employment Cost Index (quarterly) "cex" Consumer Expenditure Survey "jolts" Job Openings Labor Turnover Survey "cps" Current Population Survey "ces" Current Employment Statistics - series "ces_allemp" Current Employment Statistics - employees, seasonally adjusted "ces_total" Current Employment Statistics - total nonfarm employment "averageprice" Average price data - current "food" Average price data - food items "se" State metro area employment "su" State local area unemployment email Character string email address. Required BLS identifying API users. Set HTTP User-Agent header.

title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
text: tibble containing merged data columns : series_id Unique identifier data series date Observation date value Numeric data value ... Additional metadata columns vary data source (e.g., item codes, industry codes, area codes)

title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
text: function constructs URLs BLS flat files https://download.bls.gov/pub/time.series/, downloads series metadata auxiliary lookup tables, downloads merges main data file. Date parsing handles monthly (sources) quarterly (ECI) data frequencies.

title: Download and Process Bureau of Labor Statistics Data — getBLSFiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Download CPI data   cpi_data <- getBLSFiles("cpi", "your.email@example.com")    # Download JOLTS data   jolts_data <- getBLSFiles("jolts", "your.email@example.com") } # }

what: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
text: Scrapes Bureau Labor Statistics table monthly Current Employment Statistics (CES) revisions https://www.bls.gov/web/empsit/cesnaicsrev.htm returns tidy tibble first, second, third prints --month change total nonfarm employment (1979-present), seasonally adjusted seasonally adjusted, along revision deltas prints.

title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
code: getCESRevisions(   source_url = "https://www.bls.gov/web/empsit/cesnaicsrev.htm",   timeout = 60 )

title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
text: source_url Character string BLS revisions page URL. Defaults CES NAICS revisions page; override BLS moves page. timeout Numeric. Maximum seconds wait page load headless Chrome failing. Default 60.

title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
text: tibble one row per month columns: date Observation month (first month). year Calendar year (integer). month Three-letter month abbreviation. month_num Month number 1-12 (integer). sa_1st, sa_2nd, sa_3rd Seasonally adjusted first, second, third prints --month employment change (thousands). sa_rev_2nd_minus_1st, sa_rev_3rd_minus_2nd, sa_rev_3rd_minus_1st Seasonally adjusted revisions prints (thousands). nsa_1st, nsa_2nd, nsa_3rd seasonally adjusted prints (thousands). nsa_rev_2nd_minus_1st, nsa_rev_3rd_minus_2nd, nsa_rev_3rd_minus_1st seasonally adjusted revisions (thousands). source_url URL data scraped . scraped_at UTC timestamp download (vintage).

title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
text: BLS revisions page sits behind Akamai bot detection rejects requests R's HTTP stack (httr/curl) based TLS fingerprint, regardless User-Agent header. function therefore fetches page headless Chrome browser via chromote package, requires local installation Google Chrome (another Chromium-based browser). chromote rvest must installed use function. calendar year page published table three-row header (adjustment status, measure, print). Tables parsed rvest::html_table(), headers flattened, target columns located pattern reordering added columns BLS page silently misalign values. year-month appears one table, complete row kept. values --month changes thousands jobs. Recent months NA second third prints estimates published; revision columns NA prints exist.

title: Download CES Monthly Jobs Revisions — getCESRevisions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   revisions <- getCESRevisions()    # Average absolute seasonally adjusted revision, first to third print:   mean(abs(revisions$sa_rev_3rd_minus_1st), na.rm = TRUE) } # }

what: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
text: Returns long tibble raw inputs Federal Reserve Bank Dallas's trimmed-mean PCE inflation rate: monthly Fisher price index, nominal expenditure, real quantity, monthly price change, Fisher (t-1, t) expenditure-share weight, flag indicating whether component trimmed month tail. Users can replicate trimmed-mean rate collapsing tibble kept (non-trimmed) components month taking weight-renormalized weighted mean price changes.

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
code: getDallasTrimPCE(   frequency = "M",   NIPA_data = NULL,   alpha = 0.24,   beta = 0.31,   components = NULL )

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
text: frequency Character. Frequency code passed getNIPAFiles. Defaults "M" (monthly). Currently trimmed-mean panel meaningful monthly frequency. NIPA_data Optional pre-loaded NIPA tibble getNIPAFiles(). NULL, function downloads . alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Lower-tail trim share. Default 0.24, Dallas Fed published value. beta Numeric [0, 1]. Upper-tail trim share. Default 0.31. components Optional override component dictionary. Must tibble columns dallas_idx, name, series_code, line_no. Defaults packaged dallasTrimPCEcomponents (177 components).

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
text: tbl_df one row per (date, component) columns: date Month observation date. dallas_idx Component ordinal Dallas tech notes (1..178, 94 omitted). name Dallas Fed component name. series_code BEA NIPA series code (Table 2.4.4U). line_no BEA NIPA Table 2.4.4U line number. price Fisher price index (Table 2.4.4U). nominal Current-dollar outlay (Table 2.4.5U). quantity Real quantity (nominal / price). price_change Period--period fractional change price. NA first observation per component. weight Fisher (t-1, t) expenditure-share weight, renormalized sum 1 within full-coverage month. NA otherwise. is_trimmed Logical. TRUE component either tail month dropped trimmed mean. NA month lacks full coverage. trim_side Character. "lower" "upper" trimmed; NA kept (interior) coverage incomplete.

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
text: Weights Fisher-style: unweighted average expenditure share evaluated base prices P[t-1] quantities Q[t-1] Q[t], renormalized sum 1 within month. Real quantity computed nominal / price BEA NIPA Tables 2.4.5U 2.4.4U respectively (equivalent Table 2.4.6U per Dallas Fed's MATLAB note, available across full sample without chained-dollar gaps). Trim assignment simple rank-based version: components sorted within month price_change, cumulative weight accumulated, components whose running cumulative weight alpha flagged "lower", components whose cumulative weight adding contribution 1 - beta flagged "upper". Boundary components straddle either threshold kept (treated interior). Dallas Fed's exact fractional-boundary handling enters rate calculation , panel-builder; resulting headline rate matches Dallas Fed series within basis points. Months without full cross-sectional coverage (.e., component missing month last) weight, is_trimmed, trim_side set NA.

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
text: Dolmas, J. (2005). "Trimmed Mean PCE Inflation." Federal Reserve Bank Dallas Working Paper 0506. Dolmas, J. (2009, updated 2022-12-23). "PCE Inflation: Technical Note." Federal Reserve Bank Dallas. Atkinson, T., Dolmas, J., & Zarutskie, R. (2026). "Skewness warrants caution Trimmed Mean PCE inflation eases." Federal Reserve Bank Dallas, April 16, 2026.

title: Build the Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE component panel — getDallasTrimPCE
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Default 24/31 Dallas Fed trim   panel <- getDallasTrimPCE()    # Replicate the monthly trimmed-mean rate (kept components, renormalized   # to sum to 1):   library(dplyr)   tm_rate <- panel |>     dplyr::filter(!is_trimmed) |>     dplyr::group_by(date) |>     dplyr::summarize(       rate = sum(price_change * weight) / sum(weight),       .groups = "drop"     )    # What got trimmed in the latest month, by tail and weight:   panel |>     dplyr::filter(date == max(date), is_trimmed) |>     dplyr::arrange(trim_side, dplyr::desc(weight)) |>     dplyr::select(name, trim_side, weight, price_change) } # }

what: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
text: flexible wrapper downloads one data series St. Louis Fed (FRED) API, optionally computes one-period percentage changes, merges tidy tibble keyed date.

title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
code: getFRED(..., keep_all = TRUE, rename_variables = NULL, lagged = NULL)

title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
text: ... One FRED series IDs.  element may either Unnamed character string raw FRED ticker; column keeps lowercase ticker name, e.g.\ "UNRATE". Named character string value FRED ticker name becomes column label, e.g.\ payroll = "PAYEMS". may also pass single character vector (named unnamed) compatibility older code. keep_all Logical.  TRUE (default) performs full join keeps dates across series; FALSE performs inner join. rename_variables Optional character vector new column names (one per series), retained backward compatibility.  Supply either argument names ..., . lagged Logical scalar logical vector.  TRUE (corresponding element TRUE), series replaced one-period percentage change \((x_t / x_{t-1}) - 1\).  Recycled match number series length 1.

title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
text: tibble date column one column per requested series.

title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
text: may supply series two ways: Natural “...” style: getFRED(unrate = "UNRATE", payroll = "PAYEMS"). Named arguments give friendly column names; unnamed arguments keep (lower-case) ticker column name. Legacy style: pass single (optionally named) character vector—e.g.\ c(unrate = "UNRATE", payroll = "PAYEMS")—/use rename_variables= argument.  remains supported backward compatibility. provide names ... non-NULL rename_variables vector, function stops prompts choose single naming method.

title: Download and Merge FRED Series — getFRED
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # New interface getFRED(unrate = "UNRATE", payroll = "PAYEMS")  # Multiple unnamed series (columns become 'unrate' and 'payems') getFRED("UNRATE", "PAYEMS")  } # }

what: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
title: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
text: function downloads processes National Income Product Accounts (NIPA) data files BEA website. reads necessary register files, formats date column, uses fast stringi functions together tidyr's unnest() split combined TableId:LineNo field separate rows columns. Finally, merges datasets.

title: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
code: getNIPAFiles(   location = "https://apps.bea.gov/national/Release/TXT/",   type = "Q" )

title: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
text: location URL path BEA files located. Default: "https://apps.bea.gov/national/Release/TXT/". type character string indicating type data load. example, "Q" quarterly "M" monthly data. Default "Q".

title: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
text: tibble one row per (series, period, table line). NIPA series can appear multiple table lines, series-periods duplicated across rows; filter TableId (LineNo) analysis. Key columns: SeriesCode BEA series mnemonic (SeriesRegister.txt). Period Raw BEA period string, e.g. "2024Q1" "2024M01". date Period Date (quarters mapped final month). Value Numeric data value. TableId NIPA table identifier, e.g. "U20404". LineNo Numeric line number within table. ... Additional series metadata SeriesRegister.txt (including SeriesLabel) table metadata TablesRegister.txt.

title: Download and Process BEA NIPA Files with Fast Row Expansion — getNIPAFiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   nipadata <- getNIPAFiles(type = "Q") } # }

what: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
text: Downloads processes BEA NIPA data compute Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price indices weights growth measures. Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure.

title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
code: getPCEInflation(frequency = "M", NIPA_data = NULL)

title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
text: frequency Character string indicating frequency data: "M" (monthly, default) "Q" (quarterly). Also sets compounding used annualize WDataValue_P1a (12 monthly, 4 quarterly). NIPA_data Optional data frame. provided, used raw NIPA dataset instead loading fresh data getNIPAFiles(). Make sure frequency matches frequency supplied data, since determines annualization exponent.

title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
text: tibble one row per (date, PCE component), containing columns getNIPAFiles price-index table "U20404" (including date, SeriesLabel, price index Value), plus: PCEweight Nominal consumption share: component spending divided total PCE (table "U20405"). quantity Real quantity index table "U20403". DataValue_P1 1-period percent change price index (decimal). DataValue_P3 3-period percent change (decimal). DataValue_P6 6-period percent change (decimal). WDataValue_P1 Contribution 1-period PCE inflation: DataValue_P1 times lagged PCEweight. WDataValue_P1a WDataValue_P1 annualized compounding periods per year implied frequency: (1 + x)^12 - 1 monthly, (1 + x)^4 - 1 quarterly.

title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
text: function performs following steps: Loads NIPA data using getNIPAFiles (uses pre-loaded data). Extracts total PCE table "U20405" (series code "DPCERC"). Computes PCE component weights nominal consumption share (component value divided total PCE). Extracts quantity indices table "U20403". Loads price indices table "U20404", joins weights quantities, calculates period--period growth measures.

title: Load and Process Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Inflation Data — getPCEInflation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load monthly PCE data   pce_data <- getPCEInflation("M") } # }

what: Get Full Unemployment Rate from FRED — getUnrateFRED
title: Get Full Unemployment Rate from FRED — getUnrateFRED
text: Downloads civilian unemployment level labor force level FRED, calculates unemployment rate \(\text{unemploy\_level} / \text{lf\_level}\).

title: Get Full Unemployment Rate from FRED — getUnrateFRED
code: getUnrateFRED()

title: Get Full Unemployment Rate from FRED — getUnrateFRED
text: tibble columns: date Observation date unemploy_level Civilian unemployment level (thousands) lf_level Civilian labor force level (thousands) full_unrate Unemployment rate (decimal)

title: Get Full Unemployment Rate from FRED — getUnrateFRED
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   getUnrateFRED() } # }

title: Log-Linear Projection (data-masked, dplyr-native) — logLinearProjection
text: Fits log-linear trend log(value) ~ t calibration window projects rows /start_date. Designed use inside dplyr verbs (need pass .).

title: Log-Linear Projection (data-masked, dplyr-native) — logLinearProjection
code: logLinearProjection(   date,   value,   start_date,   end_date,   group = NULL,   data = NULL )

title: Log-Linear Projection (data-masked, dplyr-native) — logLinearProjection
text: date Bare column name date variable (coercible Date). value Bare column name positive numeric series project. start_date Date string coercible Date; start calibration. end_date Date string coercible Date; end calibration. group Optional bare column name group projecting. data Optional data frame. omitted, uses columns visible current data mask (e.g., inside mutate()) via dplyr::pick().

title: Log-Linear Projection (data-masked, dplyr-native) — logLinearProjection
text: numeric vector projection aligned input rows; NA start_date. Respects grouping group supplied.

what: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
title: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
text: Utilities retrieve tidy U.S. macroeconomic data public government data providers, returning consistent tibbles ready modeling graphics.

title: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
text: getFRED One series FRED, merged date. getBLSFiles BLS flat files (CPI, CES, CPS, JOLTS, ECI, CEX, others), merged lookup tables. getNIPAFiles BEA NIPA flat files, expanded one row per table line. getPCEInflation PCE price indices nominal-share weights growth rates. getDallasTrimPCE Component panel underlying Dallas Fed trimmed-mean PCE inflation rate. getUnrateFRED Unemployment rate built FRED levels.

title: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
text: logLinearProjection Log-linear trend projection use inside dplyr::mutate(). date_breaks_gg, date_breaks_n Date axis breaks anchored data. theme_esp Economic Security Project ggplot2 theme color scales.

title: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
text: cesDiffusionIndex, dallasTrimPCEcomponents.

title: tidyusmacro: Downloading and Cleaning U.S. Macroeconomic Data — tidyusmacro-package
text: Maintainer: Mike Konczal konczal@gmail.com Authors: Mike Konczal konczal@gmail.com

title: tidyusmacro (development version)
text: getFRED() downloads now robust: transient failures retried 3 times backoff, transport-level errors (FRED’s intermittent “HTTP/2 stream closed cleanly” resets) trigger fallback request HTTP/1.1. Bad series IDs (HTTP 400/404) still fail fast. getFRED() now parses FRED’s "." missing-value marker NA, value columns stay numeric instead silently becoming character. network layer getFRED() factored internal helper can mocked; added full offline unit-test suite plus live integration tests (skipped CRAN offline) getFRED() getUnrateFRED(). getPCEInflation() now annualizes WDataValue_P1a using compounding implied frequency (12 periods monthly, 4 quarterly). previously always used ^4, understated annualized contributions monthly data (default) roughly factor three. Monthly values WDataValue_P1a change; columns unaffected. getBLSFiles() join handling hardened: colliding non-key columns lookup files now detected dynamically prefixed file name (addition always-prefixed metadata columns), joins validate relationship = "many--one" non-unique lookup key errors loudly instead silently duplicating rows, final invariant check guarantees .x/.y columns. Output currently supported data sources unchanged. tidyusmacro now requires dplyr >= 1.1.0 (join relationship validation). getBLSFiles("su") works : LAU state/region/division lookup now requested la.state_region_division (misspelling, state_region_divison, made every su call fail 404) joined actual key, srd_code, srd_text attached output. Documentation improvements throughout: package-level help page (?tidyusmacro), examples fuller descriptions theme_esp(), esp_pal, esp_navy, documented return columns getNIPAFiles() getPCEInflation(), cross-references date_breaks_gg() date_breaks_n().

title: tidyusmacro 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-12

title: tidyusmacro 0.2.0
text: logLinearProjection() new data-masked interface designed use inside dplyr verbs: now takes bare column names (logLinearProjection(date, value, start_date, end_date)) instead data frame plus column-name strings (logLinearProjection(tbl, "date", "value", ...)). Code using old string-based interface must updated.

title: tidyusmacro 0.2.0
text: getDallasTrimPCE() downloads component-level data underlying Dallas Fed Trimmed Mean PCE inflation rate returns tidy panel trimming weights trim-side flags. getUnrateFRED() retrieves unemployment labor force levels FRED computes unemployment rate. date_breaks_gg() date_breaks_n() provide evenly spaced date breaks ggplot2 axes. getBLSFiles() supports Consumer Expenditure Survey flat files (data_source = "cex"). New dataset dallasTrimPCEcomponents maps PCE line items Dallas Fed trimmed-mean components.

title: tidyusmacro 0.2.0
text: getFRED() downloads via httr::GET() explicit user agent, fixing intermittent “HTTP/2 stream closed cleanly” failures FRED’s CSV endpoint. getBLSFiles() longer produces duplicate .x/.y columns lookup files share metadata column names; shared metadata columns prefixed lookup file name. getBLSFiles() now restores user’s HTTPUserAgent option exit. logLinearProjection() longer calls deprecated dplyr::cur_data_all(), warned every use dplyr >= 1.1.0; now uses dplyr::pick(). Calls using old pre-0.2.0 string interface fail informative error pointing new interface. Added testthat suite covering logLinearProjection().

title: tidyusmacro 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-30 Initial CRAN submission.
